name: Lemon Chicken (Chin)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove bones and skin from the chicken breasts and discard.
2. Cut each breast into fourths. Place in a shallow glass or plastic dish.
3. Mix 1 tablespoon oil, the egg, 2 teaspoons cornstarch, 1 teaspoon salt, soy sauce, and 1/4 teaspoon white pepper; pour over the chicken. Turn to coat both sides. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
4. Remove chicken from marinade and reserve. Pour oil into a wok until it reaches a depth of 1-1/2 inches and heat to 350°F.
5. Mix the reserved marinade, flour, 1/4 cup water, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons oil, baking soda, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a separate bowl.
6. Dip chicken pieces one at a time into this batter. Fry for 3 minutes or until light brown, turning once.
7. Drain on paper towels. Increase the oil temperature to 375°F. Return chicken to the wok and fry together for approximately 2 minutes, until golden brown. Turn once and drain on paper towels.
8. Cut each piece crosswise into 5-6 pieces. Place in a single layer on a heated platter and keep warm.
9. To make sauce, heat chicken broth, honey, lemon juice, corn syrup, vinegar, 1 tablespoon oil, catsup, garlic, remaining salt, white pepper, and lemon peel until boiling. Mix the remaining tablespoon cornstarch with cold water and stir into the sauce. Cook until thickened for about 10 seconds. Remove lemon peel. Pour the sauce over the chicken and garnish with lemon slices. Serve immediately.
